_about: Curated K-bus formula inputs per part, consumed by mias-io's kbus capacity calculator. The Beckhoff K-bus cycle-time formula (T_cyc_us = N_cycles × (600 + N_DI × 2.5 + N_AI × 32 + N_AO × 42)) needs per-module {channel counts, specialty bytes, n_cycles, mode multiplier} that the catalog-derived part records don't carry directly. This file fills that gap for the modules MIAS uses; extend when new parts land in projects.
_source: Beckhoff infosys.beckhoff.com/content/1033/bk3xx0/3028165003.html — formula and N_cycles ≥ 2 rule for terminals with >6 bytes data
suppliers/Wago/Controller manuals/wago_pfc200_750-8212_g2_manual.pdf §8.9.5 Table 198 — controller cycle-time bounds, output watchdog
Techdata/PLC/wago_pfc200_kbus_capacity.md — synthesis, mitigation order, empirical curve
_schema: Each module entry has: kind (digital | analog_in | analog_out | specialty_mailbox | specialty_serial | controller), and the formula-relevant fields below. Fields prefixed `_tbd_` indicate values still to be measured/cited; treat as null until closed by the corresponding NOTIF.

formula.name: Beckhoff K-bus cycle (canonical)
formula.n_cycles_rule: ≥2 if any module on the chain has >6 bytes process image (4-channel terminals or larger). Calculator should set N_cycles = max(1, max(modules.n_cycles_required)).

wago:750-8210.label: PFC200 G2 4ETH
wago:750-8210.source: suppliers/Wago/Controller manuals/wago_pfc200_750-8212_g2_manual.pdf §8.9.5 Table 198 (G2 family — same K-bus chapter as 750-8210)

wago:750-8202.label: PFC200 G1 2ETH RS
wago:750-8202.source: suppliers/Wago/Controller manuals/wago_pfc200_750-8202_g1_manual.pdf — assumed equivalent K-bus chapter; verify and amend on first project that uses G1

wago:750-658.label: CAN gateway (Layer-2 + CANopen)

process_image_bytes.default_documented_note: Not stated in 750-658 manual or WAGO TechDocs as of 2026-05-02; tracked as open gap in Techdata/PLC/wago_pfc200_kbus_capacity.md and as NOTIF-007 item 1 to mias-plugin.

mailbox_window_bytes.note: WAGO 750-658 manual line 2260: 'Set the mailbox in these operating modes to values between 2 and 6 bytes to have the greatest possible data throughput.'

Mapped.source: Convention; Mapped is the cheapest mode per Techdata/PLC/wago_pfc200_kbus_capacity.md (cyclic K-bus updates rather than acyclic mailbox routing).

Sniffer.source: TBD — open NOTIF-008 to mias-plugin. Page asserts Sniffer/Transparent are 4–10× more expensive per frame than Mapped qualitatively; not yet quantified.

Transparent.source: TBD — open NOTIF-008 to mias-plugin. Critical for MIAS: LasseMaja and (per the page §typFullConfiguration) all current MIAS racks run 750-658 in Transparent. Calculator should emit a MODE_MULTIPLIER_MISSING warning when Transparent is selected and value is null.

wago:750-658.n_cycles_rule: PI > 6 B always triggers ≥2 cycles per Beckhoff. PI=8 is the minimum that still triggers 2 cycles; the smaller PI options don't reduce N_cycles below 2.
wago:750-658.byte_cost_us_note: Beckhoff formula doesn't have a coefficient for specialty mailbox bytes — only DI/AI/AO channels. For a first-cut TIER 1 calculator, use a placeholder of 100 µs per byte for specialty modules with PI > 6 B; this is conservative and should be replaced when WAGO provides authoritative data or when empirical fitting against the LasseMaja datapoint is performed. Tracked as a TIER 2 follow-up in Techdata/PLC/wago_pfc200_kbus_capacity.md.

wago:750-652.label: RS-232/RS-485 / DMX / Data Exchange gateway

RS-232.source: Convention — no documented K-bus cost difference between serial sub-modes; treat as 1.0× until measured.

wago:750-652.n_cycles_rule: PI ≥ 8 B; minimum 8 B is at the >6 B threshold so always triggers 2 cycles. PI=24 (default) and 48 are larger.
wago:750-652.byte_cost_us_note: Same caveat as 750-658 — the Beckhoff formula doesn't have a coefficient for specialty modules. Use 100 µs/byte placeholder for PI > 6 until measured.

wago:750-455.label: 4 AI 4..20mA single-ended
wago:750-455.n_cycles_rule: 4-channel rule per Beckhoff: terminals with 4 channels require ≥2 K-bus cycles even when PI is small.

wago:750-628.label: K-bus extension coupler (head of segment)
wago:750-628.note: Mechanical/electrical hop only; does NOT reduce K-bus loading. Counted by the calculator only to enforce the with-extension module-count limit (250 vs 64) and the per-segment limits (max 11 blocks of 10 modules, max 5 m between blocks per PFC200 G2 manual §3.8.6 Table 22).
